description_long: This object is a tall, upright stone guard stone featuring a prominent Celtic cross design at the top, mounted on a square base. The cross is intricately decorated with relief carvings, including knotwork and geometric patterns, which are typical of historical Celtic monuments. The shaft of the cross is rectangular and also adorned with detailed engravings, while the base displays swirling motifs and possibly animal figures. The entire structure appears to be carved from a single block of stone, giving it a monolithic and robust appearance. The surface is rough and weathered, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. This type of guard stone would serve both as a protective barrier and a decorative or commemorative feature in an urban or historical setting.
description: A tall, intricately carved stone guard stone with a Celtic cross atop a square base, featuring detailed relief patterns and a weathered surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full face of the Celtic cross with detailed carvings and the ornate base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness of the cross and the swirling patterns on the shaft and base.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the back of the cross and base with similar engravings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the profile of the cross and additional carved motifs on the base.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, parks, or along urban sidewalks as a protective or commemorative marker, often near pedestrian pathways or at the edge of plazas.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright and securely anchored to prevent movement.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show surface erosion, smoothing of carvings, and discoloration due to prolonged exposure to weather, as well as minor chipping at the base from incidental contact with vehicles or foot traffic.
attribute_historical_markings: The object features intricate Celtic knotwork, geometric reliefs, and possible animal motifs carved into the cross and base, typical of historical or commemorative stonework.
